title: CI: must-add-tests check: use GH label, not text
body:
Old way: edit commit message, add magic string, re-push

New way: repo maintainer adds a Github label to PR, hits Rerun

I've looked and looked for the history behind this script
and why I didn't do it this way in the first place. I've
concluded that I just never thought of it.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Curious if the sekrit detector will trigger

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

>Maybe we can combine the two approaches? The old magic string could lead to auto-apply the label.

@vrothberg my understanding, based on discussion in another PR, is that there is some value to requiring repo-maintainer signoff on PRs without tests. At a very minimum it will bubble up to someone's attention, and that someone can suggest ways to test. And hence not require the label.
